summary.intent: Merge the main branch into the feature branch `feat/summary-budget-discipline` (PR #652) to resolve conflicts and push the result.
summary.outcome: Three merge conflicts in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` were resolved by introducing a `compress_summary_in_history` helper function, the code compiled cleanly, and the merge commit was pushed.

repo: The `moltis` repo uses worktrees under `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/` for branch isolation.
Compaction logic lives in `crates/chat/src/compaction_run/mod.rs` and was refactored in main to use a `run_compaction` function.
`Semaphore` (tokio), `HashSet`, and `tempfile` are already in scope in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` тАФ no new imports were needed during conflict resolution.
The branch added `compress_summary` functionality; main added `run_compaction` refactor and new skills tests тАФ both sets of changes needed to coexist.

code.finding: A new helper `compress_summary_in_history` was introduced to deduplicate compress_summary application at two call sites that conflicted between the branch and main.
Main introduced `run_compaction` and `extract_summary_body` functions as part of a compaction architecture refactor.

workflow: Resolving conflicts that involve both structural refactors (main) and new feature logic (branch) benefits from introducing a small helper to unify both call sites rather than picking one side.
Always compile after conflict resolution before committing тАФ the compilation check caught correctness before push.

friction: Multiple reads of the same large file were needed to gather enough context around each conflict region, indicating the file is long and conflicts were spread across it.
The compaction module structure required several Glob/Grep iterations to locate (`compaction_run.rs` vs `compaction_run/mod.rs`).
